title: TC001-Start stopwatch and verify it counts up
description: Verifies the stopwatch can be started from zero and visibly counts up.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click Segmented Control 'Stopwatch' tab (element index 48).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=00:00:00').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=00:00:01').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=00:00:02').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Resume after pause and verify counting continues

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)

    finally:

title: TC008-Run a preset workout and verify countdown + timeline visuals appear
description: Verifies a user can select a preset template, start it, and see the circular countdown and workout timeline during playback.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click Segmented Control 'Presets' tab (click element index 38).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click a template card to select it (click element index 142), then click the 'Start' button for that template (click element index 154) to begin playback.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Stop button to stop the active preset playback (use the visible Stop button at index 351).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[1]/button[1]').is_visible(), 'Preset Timer tab not visible'
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button[1]').is_visible(), 'Start button not visible'
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button[2]').is_visible(), 'Stop button not visible'
        # The following required elements from the test plan are not present in the available elements list:
        raise AssertionError('Circular Timer and Workout Timeline elements not found on the page (features appear missing). Marking task as done.')
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC009-Pause and resume a running preset workout
description: Verifies Start/Pause control pauses playback and can resume playback afterwards.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click Segmented Control 'Preset Timer' tab to switch to Presets view (use element index 3).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click a template card to select it (use element index 142). After selecting, click the 'Start [Template Name]' button (element index 154) to begin playback.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Preset Timer' tab (element index 293) to return to the Presets view so the preset playback controls (including Start/Pause) can be located and interacted with.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Preset Timer' tab (element index 549) to return to the Presets view so the preset playback controls (including Start/Pause) can be located.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Paused').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Paused').first).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Preset playback pause/resume verification could not be completed because the SPA rendered a blank page with 0 interactive elements after starting playback.
- Click on the Start/Pause pill failed: the element was reported non-interactable/stale (previous attempt index 258 failed) and no alternative interactive control was available.
- Repeated attempts to access playback controls failed: subsequent Start TABATA clicks returned non-interactable/stale elements (e.g., index 667), preventing re-entry to the playback UI.
- The required UI could not be recovered without reloading the same URL, which is disallowed by the test navigation rules, so the test cannot proceed to complete pause/resume/stop verification.

title: TC010-Skip to the next step during a running preset workout
description: Verifies the Skip control advances the workout timeline to the next step during playback.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the Segmented Control 'Preset Timer' tab (acts as 'Presets') to show templates
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the first template card (TABATA) to select it.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Start button for TABATA (interactive element index 154) to begin the compiled workout queue.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Start button (index 351) on the current page to begin playback so the Skip control appears, then verify timeline and perform the Skip action.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Preset Timer' segmented control (index 357) to ensure the Preset Templates are shown (if not already), then start playback using the visible Start button (index 413) so the Skip control appears.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the visible Start button (index 633) to start playback so the Skip control (⏭ Skip) appears, then verify timeline and proceed to Skip.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(normalize-space(.), "Workout Timeline")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Next').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Skip control not found on page during active playback; no 'Skip' button is present in the interactive elements list.
- Attempt to click previously-known Skip element failed due to stale or non-interactable element indices.
- Unable to verify that the workout timeline advances to the next step because the Skip control is not available.
- The Stop button is present but cannot be used to validate the 'Next' state after skipping.

title: TC015-Build a simple custom workout and start/pause playback
description: Verifies a user can add exercise and rest blocks, start the workout, see the timer/timeline, and pause/resume.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the segmented control 'Custom' tab to switch to Custom mode

        frame = context.pages[-1]

    finally:

            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/button[2]')

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/button[3]')
        assert await elem.is_visible(), 'Import (📁) action button not visible'
        # The test plan requires a 'Custom' segmented control tab, but no exact xpath for it is present in the available elements list.
        preset_text = await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[1]/button[1]').inner_text()
        stopwatch_text = await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[1]/button[3]').inner_text()
        if 'Custom' not in preset_text and 'Custom' not in stopwatch_text:
            raise AssertionError('Custom segmented control not found on page; cannot complete the test plan.')
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type 'Plank' into the Name input for the new exercise (index 95), then enter 'abc' into the Duration input (index 96), then click the Start Workout button (index 73).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Plank')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div/input[2]').nth(0)

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=invalid').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(text(),"Circular Timer")]').first).to_be_hidden(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/button[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=00:00:00').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=00:00:00').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=00:00:00').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
